report_name: Foxconn Hit by Nitrogen Ransomware, Škoda Auto Online Shop Disrupted in Separate Attacks

threat_actors.name: Nitrogen ransomware group
threat_actors.description: A financially motivated ransomware group that conducted a double-extortion attack against Foxconn's North American facility, exfiltrating 8 TB of data and encrypting files.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Nitrogen ransomware group used its payload to encrypt files at Foxconn's North American facility to disrupt operations and demand a ransom.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The Nitrogen ransomware group exfiltrated 8 TB of data, including 11 million files of confidential project documentation, from Foxconn's network.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Attackers exploited a software vulnerability in Škoda Auto's public-facing online shop to gain access, disrupt the service, and likely compromise customer data.

malware.name: Nitrogen Ransomware
malware.description: Ransomware payload used by the Nitrogen group to encrypt files at Foxconn's facility as part of a double-extortion attack.

vulnerabilities.name: Škoda Auto Online Shop Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: An unspecified software vulnerability in Škoda Auto's online shop was exploited by attackers, leading to the platform's disruption and a likely compromise of customer personal data.

identities.name: Foxconn
identities.description: A multinational electronics manufacturer whose North American facility was targeted by the Nitrogen ransomware group, resulting in the exfiltration of 8 TB of data.

identities.name: Škoda Auto
identities.description: An automaker and subsidiary of the Volkswagen Group whose online shop was disrupted by an attack exploiting a software vulnerability, likely compromising customer data.

identities.name: Volkswagen Group
identities.description: The parent company of Škoda Auto, mentioned in the context of the attack on its subsidiary.

identities.name: Apple
identities.description: A key client of Foxconn, whose data may have been compromised in the ransomware attack.

identities.name: Nintendo
identities.description: A key client of Foxconn, whose data may have been compromised in the ransomware attack.

infrastructure.name: Nitrogen Ransomware C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure used by the Nitrogen ransomware group for command and control and to exfiltrate 8 TB of data from Foxconn.
